commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 7 성능 최적화 — 시맨틱 캐시, Redis LLM 큐잉, SQLite WAL, 멀티워커

- SQLite WAL 모드: journal_mode=WAL로 동시 읽기/쓰기 성능 향상
- SemanticCache: text-embedding-3-small + Qdrant query_cache 컬렉션, 코사인 유사도 0.92 임계값
- LLMJobQueue: Redis Stream XADD/XREADGROUP/XACK 패턴으로 LLM 요청 큐잉
- LLMWorker: asyncio 백그라운드 태스크, PID 기반 consumer 명명, Citation 직렬화
- Uvicorn 멀티워커: entrypoint.sh에서 `--workers $(nproc)` 동적 설정
- api/routers/chat.py: 캐시 히트 시 즉시 스트리밍, 미스 시 큐 경유
- api/main.py: lifespan에 worker 백그라운드 태스크 등록
- README.md: 아키텍처, ERD, RAG 파이프라인, LangChain, 기술적 난제, AI 활용 개발 방법론 전면 작성
- architecture.md 제거 (README.md로 통합)
- TDD: test_db_wal, test_semantic_cache, test_llm_queue, test_llm_worker 추가

commit.message:
Add web search fallback to helper buttons when DB coverage is sparse

Chart explainer now checks if DB headlines are sufficient for the
chart's subject (country-specific for hotspots, brand-specific for
VLDS, topic-specific for topic intelligence, etc.). When insufficient,
triggers a targeted web search via NewsAPI/Google News RSS and appends
results to Claude's context. Prevents helper buttons from being unable
to explain chart signals our RSS sources don't cover (e.g., Poland
ranking as top hotspot with only lifestyle articles in DB).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix lib.load error: inline EB3Display stub via preprocessCode instead of includes

JSCPP's includes config triggers lib.load internally which fails in this
bundle version. Instead, #include <EB3Display.h> is replaced inline during
preprocessing. String literals in display calls are replaced with 0 since
JSCPP cannot handle const char* parameters.

commit.message:
feat(post-6.3): EXIF-Rotation, Health/Settings-UI, Mitglieder-Sync & Bugfixes

Fix 1 — EXIF-Bildrotation (ThumbnailManagerImpl): applyExifRotation() via
androidx.exifinterface:1.3.7 korrigiert gedrehte Galerie-/Kamera-Fotos vor Crop.

Fix 2 — Health-UI vollständig: HealthViewModel + HealthScreen + HealthUiState
in :feature:health; GesundheitRoute -> HealthRoute() (kein Platzhalter mehr).

Fix 3 — Settings vollständig: SettingsViewModel (DataStore) + SettingsScreen +
ThemeMode-Enum + SettingsModule; TierappTheme erhält darkTheme aus Settings;
Logout via FirebaseAuth.signOut(); signingConfig aus Env-Vars.

Fix 4 — Family-Code: joinByInviteCode() normalisiert trim().uppercase();
fetchMembers() lädt alle bestehenden Mitglieder nach Join lokal in Room.

Fix 5 — Branding: "VibeCode Solutions" Footer unterhalb NavigationBar.

Mitglieder-Realtime-Sync: FamilyFirestoreDataSource +fetchMembers/observeMembers;
RealtimeSyncObserver +Members-Listener; alle collect-Blöcke in runCatching.

Firestore Rules: allow read aufgeteilt in allow list (authenticated, Invite-Lookup)
+ allow get (Mitglied); EdgeCaseStressTest + androidTest-Deps in :core:database.

commit.message:
Add lore book: The Ring Counter

A tree surveyor discovers his employer — a Veiled Hand front — is
using his reports to find and destroy ancient trees whose growth
rings encode forbidden memories. He starts lying on his forms
and hiding bark rubbings.

of the compiled JavaScript. OpenClaw cannot execute TypeScript directly,
so none of the wade-tools registered — Wade had zero tool access.

- Move press_hint text ONTO the button (absolute overlay, dark blue #1A5F82)
- Logo faded to opacity 0.22 as watermark behind the 3-line text
- Remove separate .press-hint paragraph below button
- setDone() replaces innerHTML (auto-removes hint), no external ref needed

lang.js:
- press_hint now 3 lines with \n breaks in all 12 languages
  (PRESS / TO CHOOSE / PEACE and equivalents)

index.html:
- After first registration → redirect to peace.html (not map.html)

  instead of country — anonymous but unique identifier per referral

feat: autonomous coaching reactor — proactive alerts, auto-adaptation, enriched check-ins

Phase 1 of the autonomous coaching evolution:

- Add get_alerts + get_patterns tools to engine (14 tools total)
  Wire generate_alerts() and analyze_patterns() into agentic loop
  so Claude can proactively detect risks without athlete asking

- Create reactor.py — event-driven autonomous coaching loop
  Daily morning reactor (07:00): fetches wellness, runs alerts,
  assesses adaptation needs, updates Intervals.icu calendar,
  builds morning brief for check-in injection

- Post-activity reactor: when new Strava activity detected,
  compares actual vs planned (TSS/duration deviation), generates
  coaching notes, flags significant deviations for adaptation

- Context-enriched check-ins: morning check-in now receives
  reactor brief with alerts, adaptations, yesterday review,
  weekly load, and race countdown

- Proactive alert push: critical alerts (sleep crisis, HRV crash,
  overtraining) pushed immediately to Telegram with dedup (12h)

- Add update_event() to IntervalsClient for in-place calendar adaptation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ui: fix loading-screen size pop and terminal overflow artifacts

Two bugs fixed:

1. Loading screen was ~18px narrower/shorter than gameplay screen.
   Root cause: min-width/min-height used `ch`/`em` CSS units, which are
   computed from font metrics. With font-display:swap, the browser uses
   the fallback font's metrics until DejaVu loads, giving a smaller `ch`
   value (~9.41px vs DejaVu's ~9.63px. When DejaVu swaps in, the
   container jumps to the correct size — but the loading screen had
   already rendered smaller.

   Fix: replace ch/em with a pixel formula based on DejaVu's known
   advance-width ratio (1233/2048 = 0.60205078125 per em), driven by
   the --game-font-size custom property which is defined in CSS as 16px
   from the very first parse. No font metrics needed.
     min-width: calc(var(--game-font-size) * 80 * 0.60205078125 + 34px)
     min-height: calc(var(--game-font-size) * 24 * 1.2 + 34px)
   The 34px = pre-padding(8*2) + container-padding(6*2) + border(3*2).

2. Terminal could show a horizontal scrollbar (or visually appear to
   widen) when content fills the full 80-column width (e.g. the ? help
   screen). The pre element's pixel width (786.625px) exactly matched
   the container's content area; any sub-pixel rounding could push it
   1px over, triggering overflow: auto's scrollbar.

   Fix: overflow: hidden — the terminal never needs a scrollbar.

Also: font-display: swap → block for both weights, and add
<link rel=preload> for the regular woff2 in rogue and hack so the
font is available before first paint.

commit.message:
M-codex-agent-behaviour VERIFY+INSTANTIATE: enforcement retry in WS bridge

Port execution evidence counting and enforcement retry logic from
make-codex-invoke-fn (dev.clj) into the standalone WS invoke bridge.

H-1 (Q→A completeness): bridge now detects work-claim-without-execution,
task-reply-without-execution, and format-refusal — retries once with
enforcement prompt before returning to caller.

H-2 (diagnosability): every invoke-complete evidence POST now includes
executed, tool-events, command-events, enforced-retry fields. Enforcement
retry emits its own evidence entry with reason keyword.

Live-tested against codex-cli 0.111.0 — two task prompts, both H-1 PASS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace Web Audio ringtone with HTMLAudioElement for reliable stop

After 5 failed attempts to fix persistent ringtone on desktop Chrome macOS
using Web Audio API (AudioContext.close, suspend, gain muting, oscillator
tracking, kill timestamps), the sound still persisted after call accept.

Root cause: AudioContext.close() is unreliable on desktop Chrome macOS —
the audio rendering thread may continue producing sound after close().

Solution: Replace Web Audio oscillators with a generated WAV blob played
via HTMLAudioElement. audio.pause() is 100% synchronous and CANNOT fail.

Changes:
- Generate a 3-second WAV (1s dual-tone + 2s silence) as a blob URL
- Play with new Audio(blobUrl) and loop=true for ring-pause pattern
- Stop with audio.pause() — synchronous, instant, guaranteed
- Keep anti-restart guard (5s kill timestamp)
- Keep vibration and push notification cleanup
- Update unit tests for HTML Audio (8 tests passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
